chapter: Chapter 1: The First March on Rome (88-87 BCE)

questions.question: Why did the Asian Vespers massacre bind local populations to Mithridates?
options: He rewarded them with Roman citizenship and land grants
Having participated in killing Romans, they could expect no mercy if Rome returned
He threatened to execute anyone who did not swear loyalty to him
Roman law required collective punishment for communities that aided rebels
The massacre was a religious ritual that created sacred bonds

questions.question: What was the origin of the personal rivalry between Marius and Sulla?
options: Sulla had prosecuted Marius for corruption during the Germanic wars
Marius had blocked Sulla's first attempt to become consul
Sulla had captured Jugurtha but Marius received credit for the victory
They had fought on opposite sides during the Social War
Sulla had married a woman Marius had previously courted

questions.question: Why did Sulpicius originally ally with Marius?
options: Marius promised to make him consul the following year
They were related by marriage through the Cornelii family
Marius offered him command of a legion in the eastern campaign
The Senate had threatened to exile Sulpicius from Rome
He needed support for his Italian voting rights legislation

questions.question: How did Sulla's soldiers respond when he proposed marching on Rome?
options: They refused unanimously and threatened to desert
They demanded higher pay before agreeing to follow him
They agreed enthusiastically to follow him against Rome
They insisted on consulting the Senate before deciding
Half supported him while the other half joined Marius

questions.question: How did Sulla's senior officers react to his order to march on Rome?
options: They unanimously supported the decision as legally justified
All but one refused to accompany him on the march
They demanded he seek approval from the Senate first
They arrested Sulla and attempted to restore Marius's command
They agreed only after Sulla promised them eastern provinces

questions.question: What happened to Sulpicius after Sulla captured Rome?
options: He escaped to Africa and joined Marius in exile
He was pardoned after renouncing his legislation publicly
He was hunted down, killed, and his head displayed on the Rostra
He committed suicide rather than face trial for treason
He was imprisoned and later released by Cinna

questions.question: What did Cinna do after Sulla departed for the East?
options: He maintained all of Sulla's constitutional arrangements faithfully
He immediately surrendered power to the Senate as promised
He declared himself dictator and abolished the consulship
He revived Sulpicius's program for distributing Italian voters across all tribes
He negotiated an alliance with Mithridates against Sulla

questions.question: According to tradition, how did Marius escape execution during his exile?
options: He bribed his guards with gold he had hidden in his clothing
His veterans stormed the prison and freed him by force
The slave sent to kill him fled when Marius confronted him
Cinna secretly arranged his escape through underground tunnels
A sympathetic magistrate declared his death sentence invalid

questions.question: How long did the Marian massacres last before Cinna ended them?
options: A single day of targeted killings
Three days of street fighting
Two weeks of systematic purges
Five days of terror
An entire month of violence

questions.question: Which descriptions accurately characterize Cinna's rule of Rome after Marius's death? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: He held the consulship continuously from 86 to 84 BCE in violation of constitutional norms
He voluntarily shared power equally with the Senate
He governed through military force and political intimidation
He formally abolished the Republic and declared himself king
He immediately began peace negotiations with Sulla in the East

questions.question: How long did Marius survive into his seventh consulship?
options: He died before taking office officially
Seventeen days
Three months
Six months
He served the entire year

questions.question: Which factors enabled Marius to return to Italy and march on Rome in 87 BCE? (SELECT ALL THAT APPLY)
options: Sulla sent him a formal invitation to return from exile
Cinna, expelled from Rome, allied with him and provided troops
Italian communities denied their promised voting rights rallied to his cause
The Senate voted to recall him after Sulla's defeat in Greece
Mithridates provided him with warships and mercenary soldiers
